title: The Fix Global Market Update From Shipfix
section_path: The Fix Global Market Update From Shipfix
section_path_text: The Fix Global Market Update From Shipfix
summary:
Main
The Baltic Dry Index remained broadly unchanged yesterday as the capesizes and panamaxes moved in opposite directions and offset each other. Among the commodities, energy prices moved higher, while an improving supply outlook weighed on grains and oilseeds. By Ulf Bergman

Macro/Geopolitics
While holidays in China are keeping reporting from the world's second-largest economy at a minimum, the US saw a slew of mixed economic data yesterday.

children.title: Main
children.summary: The Baltic Dry Index remained broadly unchanged yesterday as the capesizes and panamaxes moved in opposite directions and offset each other. Among the commodities, energy prices moved higher, while an improving supply outlook weighed on grains and oilseeds.

children.title: Macro/Geopolitics
children.summary: While holidays in China are keeping reporting from the world's second-largest economy at a minimum, the US saw a slew of mixed economic data yesterday. Retail sales in the world's largest economy declined by 0.8 per cent in January compared to the month before.

children.title: Commodity Markets
children.summary: Yesterday, crude oil recovered the losses incurred on Wednesday as a weaker dollar supported prices. The April Brent futures rose by 1.4 per cent, ending the day at 82.86 dollars per barrel.

children.title: Freight and Bunker Markets
children.summary: The Baltic Dry Index edged down by the smallest of margins yesterday, i.e. one index point or less than 0.1 per cent, as developments for the capesize and panamax indices offset each other.

children.title: The View from the Shipfix Desk
section_path: The View from the Shipfix Desk
children.section_path_text: The View from the Shipfix Desk
children.summary: The past two weeks have seen some tentative signs of a rebound in demand for panamaxes loading agricultural commodities on the east coast of South America. Last week saw aggregate cargo order volumes of around 2.4 million tonnes.
